- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- libnn-wiki-writer: Internal style guide for writing docs/wiki/ pages during /libnn-optimizer learn phase. Not a standalone skill -- read by the optimize agent.
- mlir-transform-writer: Write MLIR transform dialect scripts that directly control IR lowering - tiling, fusion, vectorization, bufferization, GPU thread mapping, shared memory promotion, and more. Use this skill whenever someone asks to generate, write, or modify a transform dialect script, transform library, codegen strategy, or tuning spec in MLIR. Also trigger when the request involves composing IR transformations for linalg ops, controlling IREE codegen pipelines via transform dialect, or replacing annotation-only configs with full transform strategies. Covers both upstream MLIR transform ops and IREE-specific extensions.
- iree-optimizer: Agentic per-dispatch tile-size tuner for IREE CPU codegen on RISC-V. Keeps IREE's chosen pipeline (CPUDoubleTilingExpert / CPUConvTileAndDecomposeExpert / CPUBufferOpsTileAndVectorize) and only overrides the root linalg op's lowering_config<tile_sizes = [...]>. Each round reads the best-so-far RISC-V assembly (LMUL, VLmax, register spills) to ground the next tile-size proposal; pre-compile divisibility gate ensures tiles divide their parent extents (no tail loop); a post-compile check of the IR after iree-codegen-generic-vectorization confirms no tail/masked main path. Compiles via iree_tuner.py, benchmarks on FPGA, iterates. Use whenever the user mentions tuning IREE dispatch tile sizes, agentic tile search, or wants to replace GA-based tile-size search with LLM-driven search. Trigger on "iree optimize", "optimize dispatch", "tile tune", or references to dispatch .mlir files for performance tuning.
- libnn-optimizer: Autonomous optimization workflow for libnn RISC-V neural network library functions
in the agentic_libnn_opt project. Use this skill when the user wants to optimize a
libnn function for FPGA performance, run the libnn optimization loop, validate the
optimization config, parse FPGA hardware features, extract shapes from perf test files,
or manage the optimization state machine. Trigger when: user mentions "optimize",
"libnn", "FPGA perf", "optimization loop", "validate config",
"phase gather/baseline/iterate/evaluate", "perf test", or references opt/config.toml,
opt/state.json, or any libnn function optimization task.
- cc-log: Lightweight system log to record all Claude Code behavior (tool use, subagent creation, compaction, permissions, session lifecycle) for analysis. Use when the user wants to enable/disable behavior logging, analyze Claude Code session logs, list saved logs, or understand Claude Code's tool usage patterns. Trigger on: "cc-log", "behavior log", "system log", "record behavior", "log enable", "log disable", "analyze log".
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content: what's the tile size of vd4dot of path 4 and path 5

lastPrompt: what's the tile size of vd4dot of path 4 and path 5

content.text:
Both paths use the same VD4DOT chunk size: `maxvl_per_loop` set at L338 (path 4) and L549 (path 5) via:

```
VSETVLI(maxvl_per_loop, x0, SEW=E8, LMUL=M8)
```

So **vd4dot tile = VLMAX @ SEW8/LMUL8 = VLEN bytes per iteration** (e.g. 512 B at VLEN=512, 256 B at VLEN=256). The subsequent `VSETVLI(..., SEW=E32, LMUL=M8)` before `VD4DOTS_VV` reuses that same `vl`, so 4 bytes feed each i32 lane -> VLEN/4 i32 accumulator lanes updated per issue.

Path 4 and path 5 are identical on the vd4dot inner loop; they differ only in outer im2col buffering (256 cols vs 2 cols).

At VLEN=1024, SEW=E8/LMUL=M8:

- VLMAX = VLEN * LMUL / SEW = 1024 * 8 / 8 = **1024 bytes per vd4dot iteration**
- vd4dot consumes 4 bytes per i32 lane -> **256 i32 accumulator lanes** updated per issue (SEW=E32/LMUL=M8: 1024*8/32 = 256).

Same for path 4 and path 5.

Output (im2col column) tile is hardcoded, not VLEN-dependent:

- Path 4: `tiling_size = 256` output columns (L313)
- Path 5: `2` output columns (L541, L695)

Both stay the same at VLEN=1024.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=1        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    #RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        ./build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        ./build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "dx25" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "ax25" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-25-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax27" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-27-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx25v_freya" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=512 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=80"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx27v" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "n45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "d45" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "a45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "nx45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax45mpv_ace" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${PWD}/ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mp" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-zc"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "a46mpv" ]; then
    if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
        CC_TMP="${3}"
    else
        CC_TMP="riscv32-elf-gcc"
    fi

    ${CC_TMP} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    fi

    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv32-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax46mpv" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-45-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -mext-zc -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax65" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "ax66" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mtune=andes-60-series -fno-strict-aliasing -mext-vector -DENA_VEC_ISA -DMAX_VLEN=1024 -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD -fno-tree-slp-vectorize -fno-tree-vectorize"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX="riscv64-elf-"
elif [ "${CPU}" == "x86" ] || [ "${CPU}" == "x64" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX=""
else
    >&2 echo "Usage: build.sh CPU_NAME EXTRA_COMPILATION_FLAGS"
    >&2 echo
    >&2 echo "${CPU} is not a supported CPU. Please input the supported CPU name as below."
    >&2 echo "The supported CPUs are: N10, D10, D15, N225, D23, N25, D25, NX25, AX25, A27, AX27, N45, D45, NX45, AX45, A46MP, AX46MP, AX65, AX66, NX27V, AX45MPV, A46MPV or AX46MPV (case insensitive)"
    exit 1
fi

# compiler
if [ -n "${3}" ]; then
    CC="${3}"
else
    CC="${TOOLCHAIN_PREFIX}gcc"
fi

# customized optimization for 45-series
if [[ ${CFLAG} =~ mtune=andes-45-series ]]; then
    CFLAG+=" -DENA_45_SERIES_OPT"
fi

# extra compilation flags
if [ -n "${2}" ]; then
    CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${2}"
fi

# output path
LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
if [[ -n ${4} ]]; then
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${4}"
else
    BUILD_OUTPUT_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}"
fi

#==================== extra optional flags ====================
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check the zfh extension could be enabled or not with
# the extra compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by the extra compilation flags.
# -----
# check the zfh extension could be enabled or not
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check the toolchain is [single|double]-abi or not
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series
        if [[ ${CPU} == nx27v ]] || [[ ${CPU} == *45* ]]; then
            CFLAG="${CFLAG} -mzfh"
        fi
    fi
fi

INCLUDE_CFLAG="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${LIB_ROOT}/internal -I${LIB_ROOT}/libhvm-project/libhvm/include"
GLOBAL_CFLAG="${CFLAG} ${INCLUDE_CFLAG}"
RELEASE_FOLDER="${LIB_ROOT}/release"

# generate nds_version.c to make library version number
cd ${LIB_ROOT}
nds_autogen_info.sh "libnn"
if [[ $? -eq 0 ]]; then
    mv ${LIB_ROOT}/nds_version.c "${LIB_ROOT}/Source"
fi

## Path 4 (L311) vs Path 5 (L531)

Both share: identical weight-contribution pre-pass, identical vd4dot inner loop chunked at `maxvl_per_loop` = VLMAX@e8m8 (VLEN bytes/iter).

### Differences

| Aspect | Path 4 (`ENA_TILING`) | Path 5 (default) |
|---|---|---|
| Output-col batching | **256 cols** (`tiling_size=256`, L313) | **2 cols** (L541, L695) |
| im2col buffer | `256 * Cin*Kh*Kw` bytes (L322) | `2 * Cin*Kh*Kw` bytes (L541) |
| Extra buffer | `src2`: transposed kernel `Cout*Cin*Kh*Kw` bytes (L326) + `dst_i32`: i32 accumulator `256*Cout` bytes (L330) | none |
| Weight layout | Pre-transposed once via `nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_transpose_q7` (L458) | Used in-place |
| GEMM kernel | `nn_mat_mul_kernel_tiling_asym_s8_s8_s8` (L491) — wide accumulator reuse across 256 output pixels | `nn_mat_mult_kernel_s8_offset_v2` (L697) — 2-pixel pairing |
| Odd-pixel leftover | Single call with `col_counter` (may be 0..255) (L514) | Dedicated per-channel scalar-ish tail loop (L717-806) |
| Out-pixel ordering | Inner im2col fills before dispatch; pattern preserves 256-col granularity | Emits every 2 output pixels |

### Core tradeoff
- **Path 4**: trades ~`256*Cin*Kh*Kw + Cout*Cin*Kh*Kw + 256*Cout*4` B of scratch for amortized weight re-streaming — load each weight once per 256-pixel tile instead of per-2-pixel pair. Big win when `Cout` and weight volume are large relative to cache. Needs `ENA_TILING` flag to activate.
- **Path 5**: small scratch (`2*Cin*Kh*Kw`), streams weights repeatedly through every 2-column chunk. Lower memory pressure, higher weight-traffic.

Path 4 is not reachable from the default `run.sh` — `ENA_TILING` is never set anywhere in the build scripts I inspected (only appears in `internal_tool/extract_code.sh`). To hit it, pass `-DENA_TILING` via the run.sh `$1` extra-flags argument.

# v2 vs v4 GEMM (vd4dots path)

| Aspect | v2 (baseline, L1728-1874) | v4 (optimized, L2333-2492) |
|---|---|---|
| Main-loop LMUL | M4 | M2 |
| Output rows / iter | 2 (cur_row += 2) | 4 (cur_row += 4) |
| Output cols / iter | 2 pixels | 2 pixels |
| Tile shape | 2x2 (4 dot-products/iter) | 4x2 (8 dot-products/iter) |
| Accumulators | v0/v4/v8/v12 (4 x M4) - 16 regs | v0/v2/v4/v6/v8/v10/v12/v14 (8 x M2) - 16 regs |
| A (weight) loads / iter | 2 (ptr_a0, ptr_a1 to V16, V20) | 4 (ptr_a0..a3 to V20/V22/V24/V26) |
| B (pixel) loads / iter | 2 (V24, V28) | 2 (V16, V18) - **reused across all 4 weight rows** |
| MACs per inner iter | 4 x VD4DOTS on M4 | 8 x VD4DOTS on M2 (same total elements: 4*VLEN vs 8*VLEN/2) |
| `maxvl_per_loop` | VSETVLI E8/M4 (L1751) | VSETVLI E8/M2 (L2325) |
| Tail handling | same `col_count > 0` + zero-pad pattern (L1791-1817) | same pattern, 8 vd4dots (L2387-2418), plus a separate M4 2-row fallback block (L2495-2604) and M4 1-row fallback (L2607-2691) |
| Reduction epilogue | 4 VREDSUM + 2 VSLIDEUP; pack VL=2 (L1819-1829) | 8 VREDSUM + 6 VSLIDEUP; pack VL=4 (L2421-2445) |
| Quantize/store | VWMUL + VNSRA + VSSRA + clamp + VNSRA x2 at VL=2 (L1838-1867) | Same op sequence at VL=4, stores 4 bytes per pixel (L2456-2485) |
| New instruction kinds | - | None; same VD4DOTS_VV / VWMUL_VV / VREDSUM_VS / VSLIDEUP_VI set |

**Why v4 is faster (MACs/cycle):**
- Pixel-B data reuse: the 2 B-loads are consumed by 4 weight rows -> **halves B-side loads per MAC** (v2 loaded B twice per 2 rows -> 1 B-load per row; v4 loads B twice per 4 rows -> 0.5 B-load per row).
- MAC density: 8 VD4DOTS back-to-back give the scheduler a longer independent chain (8 distinct accumulator regs v0/v2/.../v14), improving pipeline fill vs v2's 4-MAC chain.
- LMUL=M2 doubles the number of simultaneously-live architectural vector registers (16 M2 regs vs 8 M4 regs), enabling the 4x2 tiling without spills.
- Amortized epilogue: one requantize/store handles 4 channels x 2 pixels vs v2's 2x2, so reduction/quantize overhead is paid half as often.
- Trade-off: more VREDSUM+VSLIDEUP in the epilogue (8+6 vs 4+2), but that cost is amortized across 2x more channels per outer iter.
